name: Torrenueva from Cózar – circular via Almedina and Torre de Juan Abad
name_original: Torrenueva desde Cózar – circular por Almedina y Torre de Juan Abad
description: Cycle a moderate 56.8-mile loop from Cózar to Torrenueva, passing through Almedina and Torre de Juan Abad, with 1812 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Torre De Juan Abad traverse the Sierra Morena region, characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain. The area features numerous hills, providing a demanding landscape for road cyclists seeking ascents and descents. Routes often follow mostly paved surfaces, with some sections requiring advanced handling due to their difficulty. The region's average elevation contributes to the diverse ascents and descents that define the cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Torre De Juan Abad The most popular road cycling route is Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop, a 45.5 miles (73.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and passes by historical sites. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almuradiel - Viso del Marqu, a moderate 43.7 miles (70.3 km) path. This route features diverse ascents and descents through the region's landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Castellar de Santiago from Almuradiel, a 51.0 miles (82.1 km) trail leading through challenging terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Torre De Juan Abad is defined by the Sierra Morena region, significant elevation changes, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with substantial climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Torre De Juan Abad's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Torre de Juan Abad?
A: There are 10 road cycling routes around Torre de Juan Abad, offering diverse experiences for cyclists of varying skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Torre de Juan Abad?
A: The region, part of the Sierra Morena, is characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain. You'll encounter numerous hills, providing a demanding landscape with both ascents and descents. Most routes follow paved surfaces, though some sections may require advanced handling due to their difficulty.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Torre de Juan Abad?
A: Spring and autumn are highly recommended for road cycling in Torre de Juan Abad. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, allowing you to avoid the intense heat of the Sierra Morena summers.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Torre de Juan Abad offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092434874/castellar-de-santiago-from-almuradiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de Santiago from Almuradiel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 51-mile (82.1 km) trail with substantial climbs. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275114242/collado-de-los-jardines-and-despenaperros-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de los Jardines and Despeñaperros Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1,150 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085974301/albaladejo-and-montiel-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 45.5-mile (73.3 km) route that offers varied terrain and passes by historical sites. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080398353/valdepenas-and-villanueva-de-los-infantes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepeñas and Villanueva de los Infantes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 80-mile (128.7 km) ride.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in both historical and natural attractions. You can find the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Mill&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5485461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest Area and Viewpoint of the Mari Sánchez Reservoir (La Cabezuela)&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque views and a place to rest.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation changes, there are options suitable for all skill levels. The guide includes at least one easy route, and some moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085915474/almuradiel-viso-del-marqu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almuradiel - Viso del Marqu&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.7-mile (70.3 km) path, offer diverse ascents and descents that can be managed by those with good fitness.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling in the area?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5485461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest Area and Viewpoint of the Mari Sánchez Reservoir (La Cabezuela)&lt;/a&gt; is known to offer scenic views and a rest area, including a place to park bikes, making it a convenient starting point for some routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Torre de Juan Abad?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the diverse ascents and descents through the region's varied landscape. The area's challenging terrain and the opportunity to explore historical sites like castles and mills are also highly valued by the komoot community.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through towns or villages with amenities?
A: Yes, many routes connect various towns and villages in the Sierra Morena region. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085974301/albaladejo-and-montiel-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes through historical sites, implying access to local amenities in these settlements. Similarly, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085915474/almuradiel-viso-del-marqu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almuradiel - Viso del Marqu&lt;/a&gt; connect different towns, offering opportunities for breaks and refreshments.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5485461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mari Sánchez Reservoir (La Cabezuela)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature in the area, offering scenic views. Routes that pass near this reservoir would provide opportunities to enjoy these water views.
tips: Cózar is located on the road between Valdepeñas and Almadina on the southeast route of Andalusia. If you cycle through the town you can stop to rest under the shade of the trees in the Plaza del Cristo and admire the Parish Church of San Vicente and the Hermitage of the Santísimo Cristo de la Veracruz around the square.
Views of the town of Almedina from the CR-6221 road, surrounded by olive groves and farmland, the tower of the Santa María Parish Church can be seen in the distance.

The Parish Church of Our Lady of the Elms, in Torre de Juan Abad (Ciudad Real), is one of the most emblematic churches in Campo de Montiel, with great historical and artistic value.
It was built on the site of a small existing chapel in 1243, a work of the Order of Santiago, whose cross-sword can still be seen in the pediments.
Declared a Site of Cultural Interest (BIC) in 2005.

Plaza de España and the Parish Church of Santiago el Mayor: the historical and monumental heart of Torrenueva (Ciudad Real).

The Church of Santiago el Mayor is the most prominent monument in Torrenueva and one of the architectural gems of the Campo de Montiel region. It is located in Plaza de España, the town's urban and social center. Its construction began around 1450 and was completed around 1550. It belongs to the Isabelline Gothic style, although it also features Renaissance elements in its ornamentation.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route, covering approximately 91.4 kilometers with an elevation gain of 552 meters, typically takes around 3 hours and 54 minutes to complete. This duration assumes a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as 'moderate' and requires 'good fitness' due to its length and 550 meters of elevation gain. While the surfaces are mostly well-paved, it's not an easy, flat ride and is better suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this a circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, the 'Torrenueva from Cózar – circular via Almedina and Torre de Juan Abad' is a circular route, meaning you will finish at your starting point.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Cózar. While specific parking details aren't provided, you can typically find parking in the towns along the route, such as Cózar, Almedina, or Torre de Juan Abad, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride, ideal for a racebike. The route features a rolling or undulating profile with some climbs, characteristic of the moderate difficulty in the Andalusian countryside.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Andalusia is generally pleasant for cycling outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) typically offer the most favorable weather conditions for cycling this route.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several points of interest. You'll encounter the Parish Church of San Vicente and Hermitage of the Holy Christ of the Veracruz, enjoy a View of Almedina, and see the Church of Our Lady of the Elms. The route also takes you through the Plaza de España and Parish Church of Santiago el Mayor in Torrenueva.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several official trails, including sections of the Sendero de las Avutardas, Cañada Conquense o De Los Serranos, and Ruta de los Molinos, among others.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a racebike route on public roads, it is not typically suitable for dogs to run alongside. If you wish to bring a dog, it would need to be safely secured in a bicycle trailer, but this is not a designated dog-friendly trail for free running.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and pathways through the region.

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns like Cózar, Almedina, and Torre de Juan Abad, you will have opportunities to find cafes, shops, and other establishments to refill water and purchase refreshments.

faqs.question: What gear is recommended for this racebike route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a racebike route of moderate difficulty and significant length, it's recommended to bring your racebike in good condition, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. The well-paved surfaces mean road tires are suitable.
